concerns.text: The plan cites 'UCI DROP' (Reading comprehension) and 'UCI Shopper' (E-commerce) as sources for a study on 'statistical power in small samples' using t-tests on binary classification. These datasets are NLP and behavioral logs, not tabular clinical/numeric data suitable for the specific statistical power analysis of augmentation techniques described. The modality and feature types (text embeddings, categorical logs) do not match the 'tabular datasets suitable for binary classification' requirement for t-test logic in FR-001. The plan admits only 3 datasets are available but fails to identify 2 appropriate tabular sources, forcing the study to rely on mismatched data modalities.

The plan states 'Only 3 verified datasets are available' and proceeds with NLP/Log datasets (HAR, Shopper, DROP) for a study requiring 'tabular datasets' for t-tests. The 'UCI HAR' dataset is time-series sensor data, not static tabular data, requiring significant feature engineering not described. The 'UCI DROP' dataset is a reading comprehension benchmark (text-to-answer), not a tabular dataset. Using these sources violates the requirement for 'tabular datasets suitable for binary classification to support t-test logic' (FR-001) as the underlying data structures are fundamentally different from the intended statistical simulation.
